articles.title: New to Lean? Start here
articles.description: Not sure where to start? This guide explains what lean is, why it works in healthcare, and how to begin — in plain English, no jargon.

articles.title: Leading Lean from the Middle
articles.description: Why a Lean transformation does not have to wait for the chief executive, and how a leader in the middle of an NHS organisation can start, sustain and spread it from within their own circle of influence.
articles.author: David Clark

articles.title: Seeing the Whole
articles.description: Why a local improvement can be a system loss, how the Engineering Better Care four perspectives help a leader see the whole system they cannot redesign, and how to improve a part without exporting waste to a neighbour.
articles.author: David Clark

websites.title: Lean Enterprise Institute
websites.description: The founding lean learning organisation — research, events, and resources on lean thinking across industries.

websites.title: Lean Enterprise Academy
websites.description: UK-based lean learning and development — workshops, research, and practical lean resources for practitioners.

websites.title: Kata to Grow
websites.description: Mike Rother's site for teaching scientific thinking as an everyday practice. Free downloadable Toyota Kata starter routines — improvement kata, coaching kata, and run charts — originally developed for organisations and now adapted for educators. Directly complements the PDCA approach used across Gemba Suite.

websites.title: Institute for Healthcare Improvement
websites.description: Global leader in healthcare improvement science — frameworks, tools, and learning resources for quality and patient safety.

websites.title: Improving Improvement — Engineering Better Care Toolkit
websites.description: A practical, question-based toolkit applying the Engineering Better Care systems approach — people, systems, design, risk and management — to health and care design and continuous improvement. Developed from the Royal Academy of Engineering report by the University of Cambridge Engineering Design Centre with the RCP, RCoA, the Academy of Medical Sciences and the Health Foundation.

websites.title: SaaSoft
websites.description: Healthcare operations management education and software — specialists in capacity, demand, and flow in NHS settings.

courses.title: Healthcare Operations Management — Foundations
courses.description: SaaSoft. Demand, capacity, flow, and queue management in healthcare settings — practical, NHS-focused foundations course.

courses.title: Introduction to Operations Management
courses.description: Wharton School / Coursera. Core operations management principles — process analysis, capacity, queuing, and variability. Free to audit.

podcasts.title: Health Care Improvement By Design
podcasts.creator: Simon-HCSE
podcasts.description: Inspiring stories of how a growing community of healthcare improvement practitioners are changing the NHS through health care systems engineering (HCSE). Audio podcasts generated from peer-reviewed case studies published in the Journal of Improvement Science and other validated sources.
